metric_value: Over 10,000 EBA RISC-Unix systems of the Y2K generation.
2004 will be a banner year for EBA replacement decisions.
[UNVERIFIED]
Worn-out RISC-Unix EBA systems lack SAN support, recent security improvements, XML, .Net, J2EE, WebSphere, and business-process improvements (compliance, sourcing, supply chain).
Dell has over 5,000 SAP customer installations in 60 countries.
Three Dell-SAP competency centers in US, Europe, Japan.

Pre-configured clustered servers, storage, backup, switches, Windows 2003 or Linux, Oracle 9i RAC or 10g, menu of planning/installation/tuning services including database & application migration; single PO to Dell.
Professional and managed services tripled at Dell in FY2003.
SMP scalability declines as processors are added due to parasitic resource contention.
2-way to 4-way Intel Xeon building blocks; Oracle RAC spreads work; minimum 2 nodes for failover; 2-way front-ends for client dialog; Dell|EMC SAN.
Two-node cluster of Dell 6650 4-way servers ~9-16% better performance than 8-way RISC, Xeon, and Itanium SMP competitors for as little as one-third the cost.
Web-services application platform integrating applications while providing extensibility with .Net, J2EE, and WebSphere de facto standards.
NetWeaver eliminates much of the EAI cost that accompanied the predecessor architecture.
Pre-sales sizing, SAP-on-Dell migration assessment, SAP-on-Dell migration, performance tuning, Unix-to-Linux migration; prime-contractor for small customers; SI partnership for large.
Dell's lack of services understanding 'is a myth' per Aberdeen research — the company packages services most needed.

Low-risk migration path; displacing SMP HW/SW maintenance may pay for new infrastructure; cluster of SMP building blocks handles failover; Linux robust enough for mission-critical; efficient scaling; zero-technology-risk x86 with upgrade path to 64-bit Xeon and Itanium.
While Unix is not dead, this announcement puts another nail in the coffin. Linux clusters have arrived for mission-critical EBA applications.
[UNVERIFIED]
The architecture for the first time makes Linux robust enough to run enterprise mission-critical applications with low disruption risk.
[UNVERIFIED]
Dell will earn a fair share of RISC-Unix replacement business, some to Linux and some to Windows.
[UNVERIFIED]
SAP has to date been a server Switzerland, neutral to all comers. After 5,000 installations, SAP is giving a nod to Dell.
9-16% better performance for as little as one-third the cost — typical Dell pattern driving value-oriented customer share gain.
Pre-planned installation reduces time, risk, complexity, paperwork, and vendors to manage — particularly attractive to mid-size businesses with fewer on-staff resources.
Dell-SAP coordinated support eliminates vendor finger-pointing.
Thousands of older EBA systems are reaching obsolescence; how should the business value of a next-generation platform be evaluated?
